# diagramkit — Full Reference

> Diagram rendering CLI & library. Converts .mermaid, .excalidraw, .drawio, and Graphviz .dot/.gv/.graphviz files to SVG/PNG/JPEG/WebP/AVIF with automatic light/dark mode support and WCAG color contrast optimization.

## Installation

All rendering engines (mermaid, excalidraw, draw.io, graphviz) are bundled:

```bash
npm add diagramkit
npx diagramkit warmup
```

For raster output — PNG, JPEG, WebP, AVIF (optional):

```bash
npm add sharp
```

### Repo Bootstrap For Agents

Use `node_modules/diagramkit/llms.txt` as the first file to read when adding diagramkit to an existing repository.

Recommended setup steps:

1. Add `"render:diagrams": "diagramkit render ."` to `package.json`.
2. Create `diagramkit.config.json5` only if the repository needs non-default behavior.
3. Run `npx diagramkit --install-skill` to create `.claude/skills/diagramkit/SKILL.md` and `.cursor/skills/diagramkit/SKILL.md`.
4. Run `npx diagramkit warmup` unless the repository is Graphviz-only.

## CLI Reference

### Commands

```
diagramkit render <file-or-dir> [options]    Render diagram file(s) to images
diagramkit <file-or-dir> [options]           Alias for "diagramkit render"
diagramkit warmup                            Pre-install Playwright chromium browser
diagramkit doctor                            Validate runtime dependencies and environment
diagramkit init [--ts]                       Create config file (JSON5 or TypeScript)
diagramkit --install-skill                   Install project skills for Claude and Cursor
diagramkit --version                         Show version
diagramkit --help                            Show help
diagramkit --agent-help                      Output full reference for LLM agents (this file)
```

### Render Options

```
--format <formats>       Output formats, comma-separated (default: svg)
                         e.g. --format svg,png or --format png,webp,avif
--theme <light|dark|both> Theme variants to render (default: both)
--scale <number>         Scale factor for raster output (default: 2)
--quality <number>       JPEG/WebP/AVIF quality 1-100 (default: 90)
--force                  Re-render all, ignore manifest cache
--watch                  Watch for file changes and re-render
--no-contrast            Disable dark SVG contrast optimization
--type <type>            Filter: mermaid | excalidraw | drawio | graphviz
--output <dir>           Custom output directory (all outputs go here)
--config <path>          Path to config file (skip auto-discovery)
--output-dir <name>      Output folder name (default: .diagramkit)
--manifest-file <name>   Manifest filename (default: manifest.json)
--no-manifest            Disable manifest tracking
--same-folder            Output in same folder as source files
--output-prefix <string> Prefix for output filenames (default: '')
--output-suffix <string> Suffix for output filenames (default: '')
--dry-run                Show what would be rendered without rendering
--plan                   Show stale plan with reasons (directory mode)
--strict-config          Fail on config warnings/invalid values
--max-type-lanes <1-4>   Max concurrent engine lanes in batch render
--log-level <level>      Logging verbosity: silent/error/warn/info/verbose (default: info)
--quiet                  Suppress informational output, only show errors
--json                   Output results as JSON (for CI/scripting)
```

Exit codes: `0` = success, `1` = error (render failure, unknown command, etc.)

JSON schema shipped at `schemas/diagramkit-cli-render.v1.json` in the npm package.

### Examples

```bash
diagramkit render .                                  # Render all in cwd
diagramkit render flow.mermaid                       # Single file
diagramkit render dependency.dot                     # Single Graphviz file
diagramkit render ./docs                             # Directory of diagrams
diagramkit render . --format png --theme light       # Raster for email/Confluence
diagramkit render . --format svg,png                 # Multi-format in one pass
diagramkit render . --watch                          # Watch mode
diagramkit render . --format webp --quality 85       # WebP with custom quality
diagramkit render . --type drawio                    # Only draw.io files
diagramkit render . --theme dark --no-contrast       # Raw dark output
diagramkit render . --config ./custom.config.json5   # Explicit config file
diagramkit render . --output ./build/images          # Custom output directory
diagramkit render . --plan --json                    # Stale plan with reasons
diagramkit doctor --json                             # Environment diagnostics
```

### JSON Envelope Contract (v1)

`--json` output is versioned:

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "command": "render",
  "target": { "kind": "file|directory", "path": "/abs/path" },
  "phase": "dry-run|execute",
  "options": {},
  "result": {}
}
```

Legacy root-level JSON objects are deprecated in this pre-release line.

### Format Guidance

Prefer SVG by default for rendered diagrams.
Use raster formats (PNG/JPEG/WebP/AVIF) only when the destination explicitly needs them,
especially email or Confluence embeds.

## Output Convention

Rendered images go to a `.diagramkit/` hidden folder next to each source file:

```
source.mermaid → .diagramkit/source-light.svg + .diagramkit/source-dark.svg
```

Output naming pattern: `{prefix}{name}{suffix}-{theme}.{format}`
Configurable via config.outputDir, config.sameFolder, config.outputPrefix, config.outputSuffix.

## JavaScript API

### Workflow Patterns (Agent-Oriented)

1) **Batch render for docs repos**
- Use `renderAll({ dir: '.', formats: ['svg'] })`
- Call `dispose()` at the end of scripts/CI jobs

2) **Multi-format publishing pipeline**
- Render once with `renderAll({ formats: ['svg', 'png', 'webp'] })`
- SVG is rendered first, rasters are converted from SVG

3) **Watch mode during authoring**
- Use `watchDiagrams({ dir: '.', renderOptions: { formats: ['svg'] } })`
- Keep process running; fix file and save to retry on failures

4) **Custom build-tool integration**
- Use `findDiagramFiles()` + `filterStaleFiles()` + `renderDiagramFileToDisk()`
- Update tracking with `updateManifest()` and cleanup with `cleanOrphans()`

### API Selection Guide

| Use case | API |
| --- | --- |
| Render from string in memory | `render()` |
| Render one file in memory | `renderFile()` |
| Render one discovered file to disk | `renderDiagramFileToDisk()` |
| Render a directory tree to disk | `renderAll()` |
| Live re-render on save | `watchDiagrams()` |

### Core Functions

```ts
import { render, renderFile, renderAll, renderDiagramFileToDisk, watchDiagrams } from 'diagramkit'
```

  render(source: string, type: DiagramType, options?: RenderOptions): Promise<RenderResult>
    Render a diagram from source content string.

  renderFile(filePath: string, options?: RenderOptions): Promise<RenderResult>
    Render a diagram from a file path on disk. Detects type from extension.

  renderAll(options?: BatchOptions): Promise<RenderAllResult>
    Batch render all diagrams in a directory tree. Outputs go to .diagramkit/ folders.
    Returns { rendered: string[], skipped: string[], failed: string[], failedDetails: RenderFailureDetail[] }.

  renderDiagramFileToDisk(file: DiagramFile, options?): Promise<string[]>
    Render a single file to disk with all format variants. Returns written filenames.

  watchDiagrams(options: WatchOptions): () => Promise<void>
    Watch for diagram file changes and re-render. Returns cleanup function.

  createRendererRuntime(): RendererRuntime
    Create an isolated runtime with its own BrowserPool (non-singleton lifecycle).

### Conversion

```ts
import { convertSvg } from 'diagramkit'         // also from 'diagramkit/convert'
```

  convertSvg(svg: Buffer | string, options: ConvertOptions): Promise<Buffer>
    Convert SVG to raster format (PNG/JPEG/WebP/AVIF) using sharp.
    sharp is dynamically imported — only required when called.

### Configuration

```ts
import { defineConfig, loadConfig, getDefaultConfig, getFileOverrides } from 'diagramkit'
```

  loadConfig(overrides?, dir?, configFile?, options?): DiagramkitConfig
    Load merged config: defaults → global (~/.config/diagramkit/config.json5)
    → env vars (DIAGRAMKIT_*) → local (diagramkit.config.json5 or .ts, walks up) → overrides.
    Pass configFile to load a specific file instead of auto-discovery.

  defineConfig(config): Partial<DiagramkitConfig>
    Identity helper for TypeScript autocomplete in config files.

  getDefaultConfig(): DiagramkitConfig
    Returns built-in default configuration values.

  getFileOverrides(filePath, config?, rootDir?): FileOverride | undefined
    Resolve per-file overrides for a given file path from config.overrides.
    Matches exact filename, relative path, or glob patterns.

### Browser Lifecycle

```ts
import { warmup, dispose } from 'diagramkit'
```

  warmup(): Promise<void>
    Pre-warm the browser pool (launches Chromium).

  dispose(): Promise<void>
    Explicitly dispose the browser pool and close Chromium.

### Utilities (diagramkit/utils)

```ts
import { findDiagramFiles, filterByType, getDiagramType, readManifest, atomicWrite } from 'diagramkit/utils'
```

  findDiagramFiles(dir: string, config?): DiagramFile[]
    Recursively find all diagram source files. Skips hidden dirs and node_modules.

  filterByType(files: DiagramFile[], type: DiagramType, config?): DiagramFile[]
    Filter diagram files by resolved diagram type.

  getDiagramType(filename: string, map?): DiagramType | null
    Resolve diagram type from a filename. Tests longest extensions first.

  getExtensionMap(overrides?): Record<string, DiagramType>
    Get the full extension-to-type mapping, merged with optional overrides.

  getAllExtensions(map?): string[]
    Return all known extensions.

  getExtensionsForType(type: DiagramType, map?): string[]
    Return extensions that map to a given diagram type.

  readManifest(sourceDir, config?): Manifest
    Read the manifest for a source directory.

  isStale(file, formats?, config?, theme?, manifest?): boolean
    Check if a file has changed since last render.

  filterStaleFiles(files, force, formats?, config?, theme?): StaleFile[]
    Filter to only files that need re-rendering.

  getDiagramsDir(sourceDir, config?): string
    Get the output directory path.

  ensureDiagramsDir(sourceDir, config?): string
    Get/create the output directory.

  atomicWrite(path, content: Buffer): void
    Atomic write (.tmp + rename).

### Color Utilities (diagramkit/color)

```ts
import { postProcessDarkSvg, hexToRgb, rgbToHsl, hslToHex, relativeLuminance } from 'diagramkit/color'
```

  postProcessDarkSvg(svg: string): string
    Fix dark SVG contrast by darkening high-luminance fills while preserving hue.

## TypeScript Interfaces

### Types

```ts
type DiagramType = 'mermaid' | 'excalidraw' | 'drawio' | 'graphviz'
type OutputFormat = 'svg' | 'png' | 'jpeg' | 'webp' | 'avif'
type Theme = 'light' | 'dark' | 'both'
```

### DiagramFile

```ts
interface DiagramFile {
  path: string       // Absolute path to source file
  name: string       // Filename without extension
  dir: string        // Directory containing the file
  ext: string        // File extension (e.g. .mermaid, .mmd, .excalidraw, .drawio, .dot, .gv)
}
```

### RenderOptions

```ts
interface RenderOptions {
  format?: OutputFormat           // Default: 'svg'
  theme?: Theme                   // Default: 'both'
  quality?: number                // JPEG/WebP/AVIF quality 1-100. Default: 90
  scale?: number                  // Scale for raster output. Default: 2
  contrastOptimize?: boolean      // Dark SVG contrast fix. Default: true
  mermaidDarkTheme?: Record<string, string>  // Custom dark theme variables
  config?: Partial<DiagramkitConfig>
  pool?: BrowserPool          // Optional isolated pool instance
}
```

### RenderResult

```ts
interface RenderResult {
  light?: Buffer     // Light theme output (SVG string as Buffer, or raster binary)
  dark?: Buffer      // Dark theme output
  format: OutputFormat
  width?: number     // SVG width in pixels (SVG output only)
  height?: number    // SVG height in pixels (SVG output only)
}
```

### RenderAllResult

```ts
interface RenderAllResult {
  rendered: string[]   // Files that were rendered
  skipped: string[]    // Files skipped (unchanged, manifest cache hit)
  failed: string[]     // Files that failed to render
  failedDetails: RenderFailureDetail[] // Structured file/code/message diagnostics
  metrics?: {
    durationMs: number
    lanesUsed: number
    countsByType: Partial<Record<DiagramType, { rendered: number; failed: number }>>
  }
}

interface RenderFailureDetail {
  file: string
  message: string
  code: 'UNKNOWN_TYPE' | 'RENDER_FAILED' | 'MISSING_DEPENDENCY' | 'CONFIG_INVALID' | 'BROWSER_LAUNCH_FAILED' | 'BUNDLE_FAILED'
}
```

### BatchOptions

```ts
interface BatchOptions extends RenderOptions {
  dir?: string           // Root directory to scan. Default: cwd()
  force?: boolean        // Force re-render, ignoring manifest. Default: false
  type?: DiagramType     // Filter to specific diagram type
  formats?: OutputFormat[] // Multiple output formats. Overrides format.
  logger?: Logger
  logLevel?: LogLevel    // Logging verbosity. Default: 'info'.
  progress?: boolean     // Show per-file progress logs during batch rendering
  maxConcurrentLanes?: number // 1-4, default 4
  includeMetrics?: boolean
}
```

### WatchOptions

```ts
interface WatchOptions {
  dir: string                              // Root directory to watch
  onChange?: (file: string) => void         // Callback after render
  renderOptions?: RenderOptions & { formats?: OutputFormat[] }
  config?: Partial<DiagramkitConfig>
  configFile?: string                       // Explicit config file path
  strictConfig?: boolean
  pool?: BrowserPool
  debounceMs?: number                      // Debounce time for file events. Default: 200ms
  usePolling?: boolean                     // Chokidar polling mode (useful for network volumes/containers)
  pollingInterval?: number                 // Polling interval in ms when usePolling=true
  logger?: Logger
  logLevel?: LogLevel                      // Logging verbosity. Default: 'info'.
}

type LogLevel = 'silent' | 'error' | 'errors' | 'warn' | 'warning' | 'warnings' | 'info' | 'log' | 'verbose'

interface Logger {
  log: (message: string, ...args: unknown[]) => void
  warn: (message: string, ...args: unknown[]) => void
  error: (message: string, ...args: unknown[]) => void
}

type DiagramkitErrorCode = 'UNKNOWN_TYPE' | 'RENDER_FAILED' | 'MISSING_DEPENDENCY' | 'CONFIG_INVALID' | 'BROWSER_LAUNCH_FAILED' | 'BUNDLE_FAILED'

class DiagramkitError extends Error {
  code: DiagramkitErrorCode
}
```

### ConvertOptions

```ts
interface ConvertOptions {
  format: 'png' | 'jpeg' | 'webp' | 'avif'
  scale?: number         // Scale multiplier for SVG rasterization. Default: 2
  quality?: number       // JPEG/WebP/AVIF quality 1-100. Default: 90
}
```

### FileOverride

```ts
interface FileOverride {
  formats?: OutputFormat[]
  theme?: Theme
  quality?: number
  scale?: number
  contrastOptimize?: boolean
}
```

### DiagramkitConfig

```ts
interface DiagramkitConfig {
  outputDir: string              // Output folder name. Default: '.diagramkit'
  manifestFile: string           // Manifest filename. Default: 'manifest.json'
  useManifest: boolean           // Enable incremental builds. Default: true
  sameFolder: boolean            // Place outputs next to source. Default: false
  defaultFormats: OutputFormat[] // Default output formats. Default: ['svg']
  defaultTheme: Theme            // Default theme. Default: 'both'
  outputPrefix: string           // Filename prefix. Default: ''
  outputSuffix: string           // Filename suffix. Default: ''
  extensionMap?: Record<string, DiagramType>  // Custom extension overrides
  inputDirs?: string[]           // Directories to scan (relative to project root). Default: scan entire tree
  overrides?: Record<string, FileOverride>    // Per-file render overrides
}
```

### Manifest

```ts
type Manifest = { version: 2, diagrams: Record<string, ManifestEntry> }
type ManifestEntry = {
  hash: string
  generatedAt: string
  outputs: ManifestOutput[]
  formats: OutputFormat[]
  theme?: Theme
}
type ManifestOutput = {
  file: string
  format: OutputFormat
  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
  width?: number
  height?: number
  quality?: number
  scale?: number
}
```

## Configuration

### Config File Formats

```json5
// diagramkit.config.json5
{
  outputDir: '.diagramkit',
  defaultFormats: ['svg'],
  defaultTheme: 'both',
  overrides: {
    "hero.mermaid": { formats: ["svg", "png"], scale: 3 },
    "docs/*.excalidraw": { theme: "light" },
  },
}
```

```ts
// diagramkit.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from 'diagramkit'

export default defineConfig({
  defaultFormats: ['svg'],
  defaultTheme: 'both',
  overrides: {
    'hero.mermaid': { formats: ['svg', 'png'], scale: 3 },
  },
})
```

### Merge Order

1. Built-in defaults
2. Global: ~/.config/diagramkit/config.json5 (or $XDG_CONFIG_HOME)
3. Environment variables: DIAGRAMKIT_FORMAT, DIAGRAMKIT_THEME, DIAGRAMKIT_OUTPUT_DIR, DIAGRAMKIT_NO_MANIFEST
4. Local: diagramkit.config.json5 or .ts (walks up directory tree) — or explicit --config path
5. CLI flags / programmatic overrides

### Extension Aliases

```
.mermaid, .mmd, .mmdc       → mermaid
.excalidraw                  → excalidraw
.drawio, .drawio.xml, .dio  → drawio
.dot, .gv, .graphviz        → graphviz
```

Custom overrides via config.extensionMap (merged with built-in map).

## Architecture

### Browser Pool
Single Chromium instance managed by BrowserPool:
- Lazy initialization on first render
- Reference counting with 5s idle timeout
- Launch coalescing for concurrent acquire() calls
- 4 dedicated pages: mermaid-light, mermaid-dark, excalidraw, drawio
- Page init coalescing for concurrent getExcalidrawPage()/getDrawioPage() calls
- Mermaid needs separate light/dark pages (mermaid.initialize() is global)
- Excalidraw and drawio handle dark mode per-render call
- Singleflight IIFE bundle cache builds prevent duplicate rolldown work
- Graphviz uses Viz.js/WASM directly (no browser needed)
- Process cleanup on SIGINT/SIGTERM

### Manifest System
SHA-256 content hashing for incremental rebuilds:
- Per-directory .diagramkit/ folders contain manifest.json
- Format accumulation: re-processing regenerates all previously generated formats
- Staleness checks: hash mismatch, format change, theme change, or missing output files
- Orphan cleanup removes outputs for deleted source files
- Missing outputs trigger re-render even if hash matches

### Dark Mode Contrast
postProcessDarkSvg() optimizes dark SVG readability:
- Scans inline fill color attributes
- Computes WCAG 2.0 relative luminance
- Darkens high-luminance fills (>0.4) to lightness 0.25
- Preserves hue and saturation
- Applied to mermaid and graphviz dark renders; drawio handles dark mode in-browser

### Watch Mode
Uses chokidar for file watching:
- Per-file debounce (200ms)
- Render mutex prevents concurrent manifest writes
- Failed renders leave output/manifest untouched
- Returns cleanup function to stop watching

## Testing

```bash
npm test           # All tests
npm run test:unit  # Unit tests only (fast)
npm run test:e2e   # E2E rendering tests (requires build)
npm run typecheck  # Type checking
npm run validate   # Full validation (lint + typecheck + build + tests)
```

<!-- pagesmith-ai:shared-llms-full:start -->
# Pagesmith — Full LLM Reference

Pagesmith is a filesystem-first content toolkit with two main packages: `@pagesmith/core` (shared content/runtime layer) and `@pagesmith/docs` (convention-based documentation).

Use Pagesmith when you need:
- schema-validated content collections loaded from the filesystem
- lazy markdown rendering with headings and read-time metadata
- framework-agnostic content APIs for React, Solid, Svelte, vanilla JS, Node, Bun, or Deno

Core APIs:
- `defineCollection({...})` to define a typed collection
- `defineConfig({...})` to group collections and markdown options
- `createContentLayer(config)` to query content and run validation
- `entry.render()` to convert markdown on demand

Working rules:
- prefer folder-based markdown entries when content references sibling assets
- follow the markdown guidelines in `.pagesmith/markdown-guidelines.md` when authoring content
- use fenced code blocks with a language identifier, one h1 per page, sequential heading depth

---

## @pagesmith/core

### Content Layer API

| Method | Description |
|---|---|
| `createContentLayer(config)` | Create a content layer |
| `layer.getCollection(name)` | Load all entries (cached) |
| `layer.getEntry(collection, slug)` | Get single entry by slug |
| `layer.convert(markdown, options?)` | Convert raw markdown to HTML |
| `layer.validate(collection?)` | Run all validators |
| `layer.invalidate(collection, slug)` | Cache-bust a single entry |
| `layer.invalidateAll()` | Cache-bust all collections |

### Collection Options

| Option | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `loader` | `string \| Loader` | `'markdown'`, `'json'`, `'json5'`, `'jsonc'`, `'yaml'`, `'toml'`, or custom |
| `directory` | `string` | Directory containing files |
| `schema` | `z.ZodType` | Zod schema for validation |
| `include` | `string[]` | Glob include patterns |
| `exclude` | `string[]` | Glob exclude patterns |
| `computed` | `Record<string, fn>` | Computed fields |
| `validate` | `fn` | Custom validation |
| `filter` | `fn` | Filter entries |
| `slugify` | `fn` | Custom slug generation |
| `transform` | `fn` | Pre-validation transform |
| `validators` | `ContentValidator[]` | Custom content validators |
| `disableBuiltinValidators` | `boolean` | Disable link/heading/code-block validators |

### Vite Plugins

```ts
import { createContentLayer, defineCollection, defineConfig, z } from '@pagesmith/core'

const posts = defineCollection({
  loader: 'markdown',
  directory: 'content/posts',
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    description: z.string().optional(),
    date: z.coerce.date(),
    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  }),
})

const layer = createContentLayer(
  defineConfig({
    collections: { posts },
  }),
)

const entries = await layer.getCollection('posts')
const rendered = await entries[0]?.render()
```

```ts
// Vite integration
import { pagesmithContent, pagesmithSsg, sharedAssetsPlugin } from '@pagesmith/core/vite'
import collections from './content.config'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    sharedAssetsPlugin(),
    pagesmithContent({ collections }),
    ...pagesmithSsg({ entry: './src/entry-server.tsx', contentDirs: ['./content'] }),
  ],
})
```

### JSX Runtime

Configure tsconfig: `{ "jsx": "react-jsx", "jsxImportSource": "@pagesmith/core" }`

- `h(tag, props, ...children)` — create HTML elements, returns `HtmlString`
- `Fragment` — render children or raw `innerHTML`
- `HtmlString` — wrapper to prevent double-escaping

### CSS Exports

| Import | Contents |
|---|---|
| `@pagesmith/core/css/content` | Prose + inline code |
| `@pagesmith/core/css/standalone` | Full layout + prose + TOC |
| `@pagesmith/core/css/viewport` | Responsive viewport base |
| `@pagesmith/core/css/fonts` | Bundled Open Sans + JetBrains Mono |

### Frontmatter Schemas

- `BaseFrontmatterSchema` — title, description, publishedDate, lastUpdatedOn, tags, draft
- `BlogFrontmatterSchema` — extends base + category, featured, coverImage
- `ProjectFrontmatterSchema` — extends base + gitRepo, links

### Export Map

| Import Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `@pagesmith/core` | Main API (defineCollection, createContentLayer, z, etc.) |
| `@pagesmith/core/jsx-runtime` | h, Fragment, HtmlString |
| `@pagesmith/core/markdown` | processMarkdown |
| `@pagesmith/core/css` | buildCss (LightningCSS) |
| `@pagesmith/core/schemas` | Zod schemas and types |
| `@pagesmith/core/loaders` | Loader classes and registry |
| `@pagesmith/core/runtime` | Pre-built CSS/JS accessors |
| `@pagesmith/core/vite` | Vite plugins |
| `@pagesmith/core/mcp` | MCP server for AI agents |

---

## @pagesmith/docs

### Configuration (pagesmith.config.json5)

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | `string` | — | Site name (header) |
| `title` | `string` | — | Browser tab title |
| `description` | `string` | — | Meta description |
| `origin` | `string` | — | Production URL |
| `language` | `string` | `en` | HTML lang |
| `contentDir` | `string` | `content` | Content path |
| `outDir` | `string` | `dist` | Output path |
| `basePath` | `string` | `/` | URL base |
| `footerLinks` | `array` | `[]` | Footer links |
| `sidebar.collapsible` | `boolean` | `false` | Collapsible sidebar |
| `search.enabled` | `boolean` | `true` | Pagefind search |
| `theme.layouts` | `Record` | — | Layout overrides |
| `markdown` | `MarkdownConfig` | — | Pipeline config |

### Content Structure

```json5
// pagesmith.config.json5
{
  name: 'Acme Docs',
  title: 'Acme Docs',
  description: 'Multi-package documentation',
  contentDir: './content',
  outDir: './dist',
  footerLinks: [
    { label: 'Guide', path: '/guide' },
    { label: 'Reference', path: '/reference' },
  ],
  search: { enabled: true },
}
```

```text
content/
  README.md                 # Home page (DocHome layout)
  guide/
    meta.json5              # Section ordering
    getting-started/
      README.md             # A page
  reference/
    api/README.md
```

### Page Frontmatter

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `title` | `string` | Page title |
| `description` | `string` | Meta description |
| `navLabel` | `string` | Override top nav label |
| `sidebarLabel` | `string` | Override sidebar label |
| `order` | `number` | Manual sort order |
| `draft` | `boolean` | Exclude from build |

### Home Page Frontmatter

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `layout` | `string` | Set to `DocHome` |
| `tagline` | `string` | Short description |
| `install` | `string` | Install command |
| `actions` | `array` | CTA buttons (`{ text, link, theme: 'brand' \| 'alt' }`) |
| `features` | `array` | Feature cards (`{ icon?, title, details }`) |
| `packages` | `array` | Package cards (`{ name, description, href, tag }`) |
| `codeExample` | `object` | Code example (`{ label, title, code }`) |

### Section Meta (meta.json5)

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `displayName` | `string` | Section label in sidebar |
| `items` | `string[]` | Manual page order (slugs) |
| `series` | `array` | Group pages into series |
| `collapsed` | `boolean` | Start sidebar collapsed |
| `orderBy` | `string` | `manual` or `publishedDate` |

### Layout Overrides

```json5
{ theme: { layouts: { home: "./layouts/Home.tsx", page: "./layouts/Page.tsx" } } }
```

All layouts receive: `content`, `frontmatter`, `headings`, `slug`, `site`.
Page layout adds: `sidebarSections`, `prev`, `next`.

### CLI

```bash
pagesmith init [--ai] [--config path]   # Initialize project
pagesmith dev [--port N] [--open]        # Dev server
pagesmith build [--out-dir path]         # Production build
pagesmith preview [--port N]             # Preview built site
```

---

## Markdown Pipeline

# Pagesmith Markdown Guidelines

Markdown feature support for content authored with `@pagesmith/core` and `@pagesmith/docs`.

## Pipeline Order

```
remark-parse → remark-gfm → remark-math → remark-frontmatter
  → remark-github-alerts → remark-smartypants → [user remark plugins]
  → remark-rehype
  → rehype-mathjax
  → rehype-expressive-code (dual themes, line numbers, titles, copy, collapse, mark/ins/del)
  → rehype-slug → rehype-autolink-headings
  → rehype-external-links → rehype-accessible-emojis
  → heading extraction → [user rehype plugins] → rehype-stringify
```

## Key Rules

- Use fenced code blocks with a language identifier (validator warns otherwise)
- One `# h1` per page (validator enforces)
- Sequential heading depth (no skipping from h2 to h4)
- Prefer relative links for internal content
- Do NOT add manual copy-button JS — Expressive Code handles it
- Do NOT import separate code block CSS — Expressive Code injects inline styles

## Supported Features

| Feature | Syntax | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| GFM tables | `\| col \| col \|` | Alignment via `:---`, `:---:`, `---:` |
| Strikethrough | `~~text~~` | |
| Task lists | `- [x] done` / `- [ ] todo` | |
| Footnotes | `[^id]` + `[^id]: text` | |
| Alerts | `> [!NOTE]`, `> [!TIP]`, `> [!IMPORTANT]`, `> [!WARNING]`, `> [!CAUTION]` | GitHub-compatible |
| Inline math | `$E = mc^2$` | No spaces inside delimiters |
| Block math | `$$...$$` | Rendered via MathJax |
| Smart quotes | `"text"` → curly quotes | Automatic |
| Em/en dash | `---` / `--` | Automatic |
| External links | `[text](https://...)` | Auto `target="_blank"` |
| Heading anchors | Auto `id` + wrapped anchor | All headings |
| Accessible emoji | Unicode emoji | Auto `role="img"` + `aria-label` |

## Code Block Features (Expressive Code)

| Meta | Example | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `title="..."` | `` ```js title="app.js" `` | File title |
| `showLineNumbers` | `` ```js showLineNumbers `` | Line numbers |
| `mark={lines}` | `` ```js mark={3,5-7} `` | Highlight lines |
| `ins={lines}` | `` ```js ins={4} `` | Inserted lines (green) |
| `del={lines}` | `` ```js del={5} `` | Deleted lines (red) |
| `collapse={lines}` | `` ```js collapse={1-5} `` | Collapsible section |
| `wrap` | `` ```js wrap `` | Text wrapping |
| `frame="..."` | `` ```js frame="terminal" `` | Frame style |

## Built-in Content Validators

- **linkValidator** — warns on bare URLs, empty link text, suspicious protocols
- **headingValidator** — enforces single h1, sequential depth, non-empty text
- **codeBlockValidator** — warns on missing language, unknown meta properties

Known valid meta properties: `title`, `showLineNumbers`, `startLineNumber`, `wrap`, `frame`, `collapse`, `mark`, `ins`, `del`.

---

## AI Setup

```bash
npx pagesmith init --ai
```

Generates CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, skills, markdown guidelines, llms.txt, and llms-full.txt.
<!-- pagesmith-ai:shared-llms-full:end -->
